name: frontend
// Why optionalDependencies: lightningcss and @tailwindcss/oxide use platform-specific native binaries shipped as separate npm packages with `os`/`cpu` constraints. When `package-lock.json` is regenerated on macOS, npm has a known bug where it omits the Linux platform variants from the lock file (https://github.com/npm/cli/issues/4828). Then `npm clean-install` on Cloudflare's Linux build skips them, and `next build` crashes with `Cannot find module '../lightningcss.linux-x64-gnu.node'`. Pinning the Linux variants here forces them into the lock file. They install on Linux CI and are skipped on macOS dev per their own OS constraint.
